title: Project input
description: Comprehensive structured intake for a Keystone project. Every field is optional except that at least one of `title` or `description` (under meta or top-level) must be present: Keystone must tolerate incomplete input and fill gaps as OQ-/ASM- rather than blocking (see intake.md). Structured fields are authoritative; absent fields are normal.

meta.description: Top-level metadata about the intake itself.

mode.description: Optional run mode hint for the skill (controls depth/output set).

profile.description: Optional project-profile hint (e.g. 'greenfield-library', 'integration', 'research-heavy'). Keystone may override after analysis.

title.description: Project title (convenience top-level alias for meta.title).

description.description: Free-form project description / pasted brief. Often the only field present; Keystone mines it for everything else.

functional_requirements.description: Functional requirements. May be plain strings (prose) or partial requirement objects; normalized to requirement.schema.json items during intake.

non_functional_requirements.description: Non-functional requirements (performance, security, usability, etc.). Strings or partial objects.

technical_constraints.description: Technical bounding conditions (platforms, languages, performance ceilings).

organizational_constraints.description: Organizational/process constraints (team, budget approvals, ways of working).

regulatory_constraints.description: Regulatory/compliance constraints (GDPR, HIPAA, accessibility law, etc.).

existing_systems.description: Existing systems/environments the solution must integrate with or replace.

role.description: How it relates (integrate, replace, depend on).

preferred_technologies.description: Technologies the stakeholder prefers (preferences, not hard requirements; separated from must-haves during intake).

prohibited_technologies.description: Technologies that must NOT be used.

known_risks.description: Risks already known to the stakeholder. Strings or partial risk objects.

known_decisions.description: Decisions already made (treated as Approved inputs unless flagged otherwise). Strings or partial decision objects.

unknowns.description: Ambiguities/unknowns the stakeholder is aware of (seed for OQ-).

expected_deliverables.description: Outputs the project is expected to produce.

target_users.description: Intended users / personas of the solution.

stakeholders.description: People/roles with a stake. Strings or partial stakeholder objects (STK-).

available_infrastructure.description: Infrastructure available for build/run (cloud accounts, CI, hosting, hardware). Free-form string or structured object.

execution_agent_constraints.description: Constraints on the implementing agent (allowed tools, autonomy level, review gates, sandboxing).

repository_constraints.description: Constraints on the target repository (mono/poly-repo, branching, license, existing structure to respect).

time_budget_resource_constraints.description: Time, budget, and resource limits (deadlines, headcount, spend ceilings).

out_of_scope.description: Explicitly out-of-scope items, to prevent scope creep.

additional_context.description: Any other relevant context not covered above.

$comment: anyOf enforces the minimal floor: a title, a description, or a meta block (which itself should carry a title). Everything else is optional so partial intake validates.
